description: Versioned ontology specification for World Religion Web. Schema changes require a version bump and PR approval.

governance.change_control: Schema changes must be proposed as structured JSON and approved via pull request.

entity_types.name: religion
entity_types.description: A broad religious tradition or faith.

entity_types.name: denomination
entity_types.description: A formal branch within a religion.

entity_types.name: sect
entity_types.description: A smaller subgroup or sectarian formation.

entity_types.name: mystical_order
entity_types.description: An initiatory or contemplative order.

entity_types.name: person
entity_types.description: A historical, legendary, or doctrinally important person.

entity_types.name: text
entity_types.description: A textual work, canon, scripture, or treatise.

entity_types.name: deity
entity_types.description: A divine being or sacred figure.

entity_types.name: concept
entity_types.description: A doctrinal, philosophical, or theological concept.

entity_types.name: ritual
entity_types.description: A ritual act or ceremony.

entity_types.name: site
entity_types.description: A sacred, historical, or contested site.

entity_types.name: movement
entity_types.description: A social, theological, or reform movement.

entity_types.name: event
entity_types.description: A historical or legendary event.

entity_types.name: practice
entity_types.description: A recurring devotional, legal, or spiritual practice.

entity_types.name: prophecy_text
entity_types.description: A text framed around prophetic claims or predictions.

entity_types.name: accusation
entity_types.description: A polemical accusation or charge made by one group about another.

entity_types.name: transmission_event
entity_types.description: An event where teachings, texts, or rites are transmitted.

entity_types.name: disputed_attribution
entity_types.description: A contested authorship or attribution record.

entity_types.name: polity
entity_types.description: A political structure with religious significance.

entity_types.name: legal_tradition
entity_types.description: A school or stream of legal interpretation.

entity_types.name: lineage
entity_types.description: A succession chain or initiatory lineage.

entity_types.name: artifact
entity_types.description: A physical object with religious relevance.

entity_types.name: institution
entity_types.description: An organized body such as a monastery, church, or academy.

entity_types.name: council
entity_types.description: A formal religious council or synod.

entity_types.name: cosmological_concept
entity_types.description: A concept about the structure of the cosmos.

entity_types.name: initiatory_grade
entity_types.description: A rank or grade within an initiatory system.

relationship_types.name: founded
relationship_types.description: Indicates that a person or movement founded a target entity.

relationship_types.name: founded_by
relationship_types.description: Reverse form of founded.

relationship_types.name: influenced
relationship_types.description: Indicates substantive doctrinal or cultural influence.

relationship_types.name: influenced_by
relationship_types.description: Reverse form of influenced.

relationship_types.name: split_from
relationship_types.description: Shows historical divergence from a parent body.

relationship_types.name: located_in
relationship_types.description: Places an entity in a geography or site.

relationship_types.name: associated_with
relationship_types.description: A non-exclusive associative relationship.

relationship_types.name: practices
relationship_types.description: Connects an entity to a ritual or practice.

relationship_types.name: mentions
relationship_types.description: Connects a text to a referenced entity.

relationship_types.name: worships
relationship_types.description: Connects a community or text to a deity or sacred figure.

relationship_types.name: opposes
relationship_types.description: Captures adversarial or polemical opposition.

relationship_types.name: succeeds
relationship_types.description: Temporal succession relation.

relationship_types.name: precedes
relationship_types.description: Temporal precedence relation.

relationship_types.name: has_text
relationship_types.description: Associates an entity with a text.

relationship_types.name: has_site
relationship_types.description: Associates an entity with a site.

relationship_types.name: has_ritual
relationship_types.description: Associates an entity with a ritual.

relationship_types.name: has_practice
relationship_types.description: Associates an entity with a practice.

relationship_types.name: has_concept
relationship_types.description: Associates an entity with a concept.

relationship_types.name: has_lineage
relationship_types.description: Associates an entity with a lineage.

relationship_types.name: preserves
relationship_types.description: Indicates preservation or custodianship.

relationship_types.name: transmits
relationship_types.description: Indicates transmission across entities or periods.

relationship_types.name: mistranslated_into
relationship_types.description: Marks a disputed or consequential mistranslation.

relationship_types.name: polemically_inverted
relationship_types.description: Indicates a polemical inversion or reversal.

relationship_types.name: liturgically_absorbed
relationship_types.description: Indicates absorption into liturgical use.

relationship_types.name: suppressed_by
relationship_types.description: Indicates suppression by another body.

relationship_types.name: claims_fulfillment_of
relationship_types.description: Captures explicit claims of fulfilling another tradition or text.

relationship_types.name: claims_corruption_of
relationship_types.description: Captures claims that another tradition corrupted an earlier one.

relationship_types.name: mystically_interprets
relationship_types.description: Indicates mystical or esoteric interpretation of a target.

relationship_types.name: splits_into
relationship_types.description: Indicates downstream branching into multiple traditions or bodies.

relationship_types.name: merges_into
relationship_types.description: Indicates consolidation into another body.

relationship_types.name: contests_site_with
relationship_types.description: Indicates contestation over a sacred or historical site.

relationship_types.name: initiated_by
relationship_types.description: Indicates initiation by a person, lineage, or order.

founded.notes: Use when the source actively establishes the target.

contests_site_with.notes: Prefer separate site records plus claims when possible.

field_semantics.entity.subtype: Narrower specialization within a top-level entity type.
field_semantics.entity.visibility_level: Controls whether a record is public, sensitive, or restricted.
field_semantics.entity.status: Lifecycle state for content ingestion and governance.
field_semantics.relationship.temporal_scope: Machine-readable temporal metadata used by timeline views.
field_semantics.claim.dispute_status: Tracks whether a claim is accepted, contested, or debunked.
field_semantics.source.primary_or_secondary: Distinguishes primary, secondary, and tertiary materials.
field_semantics.relationship_constraints: Defines valid source and target type combinations for each edge type.

temporal_rules: Use exact years when evidence supports them.
Use decade precision when only approximate modern dating is available.
Use century precision for broad premodern ranges.
Use era precision for named eras that are meaningful but not numerically exact.
Use mythic precision for sacred or cosmological chronology not intended as historical dating.
Timeline UIs should sort exact, decade, and century values numerically, place era values in named era bands, and separate mythic records into a non-linear lane.
